title: Three-Layer File Structure for Knowledge Base Projects
rules: Separate project into three layers: src/ (code), knowledge_library/ (public knowledge), runtime/ (private context).
src/ contains all application code: main.py, routers/, middleware/, models.py.
knowledge_library/ contains cubes/ (basic/ and experience/) and anketa/ (project profiles).
runtime/ contains personas/, sessions/, storage/ for dynamic private data.
Add infrastructure/ for Docker, nginx, requirements.txt.
Create archive/ for old files: tests/, scripts/, benchmarks/, backups/.
Never delete old files during refactoring. Move them to archive/.
rationale: Mixing code, data, and context creates chaos. Three layers enable independent evolution of each component.
examples: SKV project root: 67 files -> 6 directories (src, knowledge_library, runtime, infrastructure, archive, consultations).

anketa_context.session_summary: Night refactoring. Organized 67 files in root into clean three-layer structure.
